title: Authors and Citation
text: Marco Ferrari. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ferrari M (2026). ClockCyteR.spatial: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis circadian data. R package version 0.1.0, https://cabajr.github.io/ClockCyteR.spatial/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ClockCyteR.spatial: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data},   author = {Marco Ferrari},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://cabajr.github.io/ClockCyteR.spatial/}, }

title: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data
text: ClockCyteR.spatial R package spatiotemporal analysis circadian rhythms multichannel live-imaging time series. Starting fluorescence intensity data extracted user-defined regions interest, estimates circadian parameters (period, phase, amplitude) single-cell resolution, maps spatially, computes network-level synchrony metrics. package developed organotypic Suprachiasmatic Nucleus (SCN) slices applicable spatially resolved oscillatory time series.

title: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data
text: can install development version ClockCyteR.spatial GitHub:

title: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data
text: using ClockCyteR.spatial, raw multichannel TIFF time series must preprocessed FIJI/ImageJ generate required _results folder structure. set ImageJ macros step available companion repository: ClockCyteR.FIJI macros run order: 1.Rotate_expand_stack — align crop stacks consistent orientation 2.Extract_hemislices — extract left/right SCN hemislices full stack 3.Define_ROI — define SCN nucleus ROI extract intensity profiles 4.Extract_cellgrid_cycle — create measurement grid extract per-cell intensities Steps 3 4 generate _results/ folders consumed index_files().

title: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data
text: use ClockCyteR.spatial research, please cite: Ferrari et al. (2026, Advanced Science). high-throughput live imaging platform investigate circuit-dependent regulation circadian rhythms brain tissue.

title: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data
text: template run code using toy dataset available .
code: # Copy the toy dataset to a writable location (avoids writing into the package folder) base_dir <- setup_example()           # or setup_example("~/my_analysis") for a persistent copy  params <- make_params(   channels = list(     Ch1 = list(       enabled = FALSE,       label = "red_channel",       grid_file = "Ch1_1_grid_vals.csv"     ),     Ch2 = list(       enabled = TRUE,       label = "Syn-Axon-GCaMP6s",       grid_file = "Ch2_2_grid_vals.csv"     ),     Ch3 = list(       enabled = FALSE,       label = "Brightfield",       grid_file = "Ch3_3_grid_vals.csv"     )   ),   coherence = TRUE,   normalize_phase = TRUE,   time_res = 0.5,   intervals = list("interval1" = c(0, 72)),   time_window = TRUE,   pixel_fct = 2.82,   plotting = list(     y_limits_from_previous = FALSE,     align = list("Ch2" = "12"),     colors = list(       Ch2 = "darkgreen"     )   ),   folder_structure = list(     base_dir = base_dir # change to your actual folder when using your own data   ) )  # index files #### file_rows <- index_files(paths = params$paths)  # analyze project #### analysis_results <- analyze_project(file_rows = file_rows,                                     params = params)  # calculate plot ranges ranges <- ranges_calculation(params = params, file_rows = file_rows) params$plotting$ranges <- ranges  # Visualization ####  # generate plots generate_plots(file_rows =  file_rows, params = params)  # pull plots in a simple folder pull_plots(params = params, file_rows = file_rows)  # generate file reports generate_reports(params = params, file_rows = file_rows) generate_plot_type_reports(params = params, file_rows = file_rows)

Defaults FALSE. merge Logical; reserved future use. Defaults FALSE.

what: ClockCyteR.spatial: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data — ClockCyteR.spatial-package
title: ClockCyteR.spatial: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data — ClockCyteR.spatial-package
text: ClockCyteR.spatial package enables spatiotemporal analysis timeseries data extracted timelapse images across multiple channels. performs multivariate analysis circadian parameters starting period, phase, amplitude defined ROIs can calculate network features (optimised Suprachiasmatic Nucleus organotypic slices).

title: ClockCyteR.spatial: ClockCyteR.spatial implements spatial analysis of circadian data — ClockCyteR.spatial-package
text: Maintainer: Marco Ferrari m.ferrari20@imperial.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Run FFT-NLLS period analysis on a fluorescence trace matrix — computePeriod
title: Run FFT-NLLS period analysis on a fluorescence trace matrix — computePeriod
text: Run FFT-NLLS period analysis fluorescence trace matrix

title: Run FFT-NLLS period analysis on a fluorescence trace matrix — computePeriod
code: computePeriod(   df,   excludeNC = FALSE,   top = 30,   bottom = 18,   save.trace = FALSE,   rm.start = 0,   prep_tbl = TRUE,   preprocess = TRUE,   transp = TRUE,   add_t = TRUE,   time_res = 0.5,   smooth = TRUE,   invert = FALSE,   filterRAE = NULL,   ... )

title: Run FFT-NLLS period analysis on a fluorescence trace matrix — computePeriod
text: df Numeric matrix data frame fluorescence traces (cells × frames), long-format table prep_tbl = FALSE. excludeNC Logical; TRUE, excludes non-circadian cells returned table. Defaults FALSE. top Numeric; upper period bound hours. Defaults 30. bottom Numeric; lower period bound hours. Defaults 18. save.trace Logical; whether store individual fitted traces. Defaults FALSE. rm.start Integer; number initial time points discard analysis. Defaults 0. prep_tbl Logical; TRUE, calls prep_table() reshape df analysis. Defaults TRUE. preprocess Logical; TRUE, smooths detrends traces table preparation. Defaults TRUE. transp Logical; TRUE, transposes df table preparation. Defaults TRUE. add_t Logical; TRUE, adds time column table preparation. Defaults TRUE. time_res Numeric; temporal resolution hours per frame. Defaults 0.5. smooth Logical; TRUE, uses smoothed trace analysis. Defaults TRUE. invert Logical; TRUE, inverts fluorescence signal analysis. Defaults FALSE. filterRAE Numeric threshold Relative Amplitude Error; cells RAE value excluded. Pass NULL disable filtering. Defaults NULL. ... Currently unused.

title: Run FFT-NLLS period analysis on a fluorescence trace matrix — computePeriod
text: named list elements period_table (data frame per-cell results), period_table_unfiltered, optionally traces fit_traces.

text: file_id Character; file identifier. params List analysis parameters. base_dir Character; base directory project. intervals Named list analysis intervals. channels Character vector channel identifiers. plot_types Character vector plot-type keys include report. storage_fold Character; subdirectory containing plots. Defaults "plots". plot_report_dir Character string giving output directory rendered reports. Defaults file.path(params$paths$base_dir,   "reports", "plot_reports"). pdf Logical; TRUE render PDF reports instead HTML. Requires magick, tinytex, TinyTeX installation (tinytex::install_tinytex()). Defaults FALSE.

code: make_params(   channels = list(),   coherence = TRUE,   network = TRUE,   normalize_phase = NULL,   time_res,   intervals = NULL,   time_window = FALSE,   pixel_fct,   saving = TRUE,   reload_period = FALSE,   plotting = list(y_limits_from_previous = TRUE, previous_exp = "path/to/previous/exp",     override_ranges = FALSE, ranges = list(), plot_types = c("period", "amplitude",     "RAE", "AUC", "phase", "phase_norm", "3D"), save_format = "svg", colors = list(Ch1 =     "darkgreen", Ch2 = "purple", Ch3 = "#e5e372", Ch4 = "#00aefe")),   folder_structure = list(base_dir = "results", interval_dir = "interval", channel_dir =     "channel") )

title: make_params — make_params
text: channels named list channel definitions. element contain minimum enabled (logical), label (character), invert (logical), grid_file (character). coherence Logical; whether compute spatial coherence metrics. Defaults TRUE. network Logical; whether perform network analysis. Defaults TRUE. normalize_phase Character string naming channel use phase reference normalisation, NULL skip. Defaults NULL. time_res Numeric; temporal resolution data minutes per frame. intervals named list numeric vectors length 2 defining time intervals (start end units time_res), NULL. time_window Logical; whether subset data defined intervals. Defaults FALSE. pixel_fct Numeric; scaling factor converting pixel units physical units. saving Logical; whether save outputs disk. Defaults TRUE. reload_period Logical; whether reload previously computed period results cache. Defaults FALSE. plotting named list plotting options. See Details. folder_structure named list element base_dir specifying root output directory.

title: make_params — make_params
text: named list validated analysis parameters, defaults applied unspecified plotting options.

code: threeDplot(   data,   x_var,   y_var,   z_var,   color_var,   col_palette = NULL,   continuous = FALSE,   x_lab = "x",   y_lab = "y",   z_lab = "z",   title = "",   xrange = NULL,   yrange = NULL,   zrange = NULL,   eye = "pos1",   zoom = 1 )

title: Interactive 3D scatter plot via plotly — threeDplot
text: data Data frame containing variables plot. x_var Character; column name X axis. y_var Character; column name Y axis. z_var Character; column name Z axis. color_var Character; column name mapped point color. col_palette Character vector colors color scale; NULL use plotly default. Defaults NULL. continuous Logical; TRUE, treats color_var continuous scale; FALSE, discrete factor. Defaults FALSE. x_lab Character; X-axis label. Defaults "x". y_lab Character; Y-axis label. Defaults "y". z_lab Character; Z-axis label. Defaults "z". title Character; plot title. Defaults "". xrange Numeric vector length 2 X-axis limits, NULL automatic. Defaults NULL. yrange Numeric vector length 2 Y-axis limits, NULL. Defaults NULL. zrange Numeric vector length 2 Z-axis limits, NULL. Defaults NULL. eye Character preset ("pos1", "pos2", "pos3") named list x, y, z camera position. Defaults "pos1". zoom Numeric; camera zoom factor. Defaults 1.

title: Interactive 3D scatter plot via plotly — threeDplot
text: plotly figure object.

code: traces_correlation(   method = "combined",   traces,   network_dir,   filename,   interval_name = "Full",   grid_coord,   cell_nodes,   plot_matrix = TRUE,   filter_nonrhythmic = FALSE )

title: collection of funtions used in network analysis Compute pairwise cell correlations and return edge list — traces_correlation
text: method Character; correlation method, either "spearman" "combined" (Spearman + FFT-based rhythm similarity). Defaults "combined". traces Numeric matrix cell fluorescence traces (cells × frames). network_dir Character; directory saving correlation matrix plots. filename Character; file identifier used plot filenames. interval_name Character; interval label used plot filenames. Defaults "Full". grid_coord Data frame grid coordinates columns X Y, used spatial weighting "combined" mode. cell_nodes Data frame node metadata; must contain name column matching row indices traces. plot_matrix Logical; whether save heatmap correlation matrix. Defaults TRUE. filter_nonrhythmic Logical; whether remove edges NA weights returning. Defaults FALSE.

title: collection of funtions used in network analysis Compute pairwise cell correlations and return edge list — traces_correlation
text: data frame edges columns , , weight (Fisher z-transformed correlation).
